howItWorks.intro: This percentage calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: % Change
rules.explanation: Percentage increase or decrease from original.

howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.question: How to calculate percentage change?
faq.answer: (New - Old) / Old × 100 gives percentage increase or decrease.

faq.question: What is the difference between change and difference?
faq.answer: Change is relative (%), difference is absolute (same unit as the values).

faq.question: Is the Percentage Change Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard financial planning formulas trusted by individuals and families. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What original value should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate original value value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Percentage Change Calculator?
sections.body: The Percentage Change Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals and families who need quick, accurate calculations in the financial planning space. By entering your original value, new value, you get instant results including percentage change, absolute difference, new is x% of original. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting percentage change right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In financial planning, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals and families worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to manage your finances with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your original value and need to find the right percentage change. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Original Value
howToSteps.description: Type or select your original value in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your New Value
howToSteps.description: Type or select your new value in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your percentage change, absolute difference, new is x% of original. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your original value before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both percentage change and absolute difference — they work together to give you the full picture.
